reasoning: While text fidelity and content recall are high (0.893+), the loss of visual context is significant for an instructional presentation. Multiple instructional UI screenshots and descriptive icons are missing across ten different pages, which are likely critical for user understanding. Given the high cost of publishing broken instructional materials, human review is required to evaluate the impact of these missing visuals.

concerns.analysis: Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing or improperly rendered in the HTML output. 1) On Page 2, the headshot of Josh Meredith and two of the three personal photos are missing, replaced by a large solid green rectangle. 2) Instructional UI screenshots present on pages 6, 7, 12, 13, 17, 18, 20, and 23 are completely absent from the HTML output, leaving only the descriptive text. 3) The icons in the prompt grid on Page 24 are missing, and another large green block appears at the
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2 contains a circular headshot and three rectangular photos. Pages 6, 7, 12, 13, 17, 18, 20, 23 contain instructional screenshots of the Word UI. Page 24 contains 12 unique icons for prompt categories.
concerns.output_quote: The Josh Meredith section is missing the headshot and two photos, showing a large green rectangle instead. The sections corresponding to pages 6, 7, 12, 13, 17, 18, 20, and 23 have no screenshots. The 'Copilot Word Prompts' section (page 24) is missing icons and has a green rectangle at the bottom.
concerns.location: Sections: Josh Meredith, How to use: Copilot in Word in the document, How to use: Copilot in Word in the Copilot chat pane, Practice sections, Copilot Word Prompts.

action_items: Assess whether the missing UI screenshots are necessary for the user to follow the training instructions.
Check if alternative text for these missing images exists or if the conversion needs to be manually corrected to include the visuals.
